mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion that drives corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a p-value below 0.05 in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term coefficient turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and impending monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration in late-cycle regimes produces positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of cases, or the predictive coefficient loses statistical significance when regime filters are applied.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 210 reflects entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 210, or the signal yields a negative information coefficient over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to achieve a positive out-of-sample hit rate above 52 percent, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth during a post-shock recovery phase indicates successful economic stabilization that restores corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth in post-shock recovery periods yields negative 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of observations, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output that directly increases corporate earnings and produces positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient, or the signal generates a negative cumulative return over a 12-month forward testing period.
